title: Warm Mexican Corn Tlayuda
description: A buttery, crispy tlayuda inspired by Yucatecan flavors, topped with coconut cream, lemongrass, avocado, and fresh herbs, perfect for a no-onion, no-garlic-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat a dry skillet over medium heat and toast the corn tortillas until golden and crisp, about 1 minute per side.
While the tortillas toast, melt the butter in a small saucepan and whisk in the coconut cream, lemongrass juice, lime zest, and a pinch of salt until the mixture is smooth.
Spread a thin layer of the coconut-cream mixture over each toasted tortilla, leaving a small border around the edges.
Sprinkle shredded queso fresco evenly across the tortillas.
Layer avocado slices, shredded lettuce, and chopped cilantro on top of the cheese.
Drizzle the remaining coconut-cream mixture over the toppings and sprinkle toasted coconut flakes for added crunch.
Gently press the tortillas with a spatula to help the toppings adhere as the cheese melts slightly.
Serve warm, optionally with a wedge of lime for squeezing.
